---
title: "Chapter 9: Recruitment and Retention of Study Participants"
description: "Chapter-level study notes for Handbook of Practical Program Evaluation."
book_id: "handbook-practical-program-evaluation"
collection_id: "evaluation-texts"
note_type: "Chapter"
chapter_number: 9
source_pages: "238-267"
citation_key: "newcomer_hatry_wholey_2015_handbook_practical_program_evaluation"
evidence_ids:
  - "chunk:handbook-practical-program-evaluation:ch09-chapter-9-recruitment-and-retention-of-study-participants:c0001"
  - "chunk:handbook-practical-program-evaluation:part-1:c0001"
source_status: "needs_review"
---

# Chapter 9: Recruitment and Retention of Study Participants

<div class="chapter-meta"><span>238-267</span><span>Part 1: Evaluation Planning and Design</span><span>Source status: needs_review</span></div>

## Core Claim

Recruitment and retention are design-critical because missing or unrepresentative participants can undermine evidence quality.

## Summary

The chapter identifies common overconfidence about how many participants can be recruited and retained.

It covers procedures for obtaining participants with needed characteristics and keeping them engaged through data collection.

It also flags institutional review, burden, incentives, and tracking as practical constraints that affect sample quality.

## Key Concepts

- **recruitment and retention**: Procedures for obtaining and keeping study participants through data collection.
- **data collection plan**: The operational plan linking evaluation questions to information sources, instruments, fieldwork, and staffing.
- **use**: Application of evaluation evidence to decisions, improvement, accountability, or learning.

## Practice Implications

- Budget more time for recruitment than optimistic plans suggest.
- Track retention risk as an evidence-quality issue.
- Align recruitment methods with ethics and participant burden.

## Source Records

- `chunk:handbook-practical-program-evaluation:ch09-chapter-9-recruitment-and-retention-of-study-participants:c0001`
- `chunk:handbook-practical-program-evaluation:part-1:c0001`

## References

These notes summarise source-linked records from *Handbook of Practical Program Evaluation* [@newcomer_hatry_wholey_2015_handbook_practical_program_evaluation].
